steps.title: Understand what f(w) = 6w² represents
steps.content: Width = $w$ ft, length = $6w$ ft (6 times the width). Area = width × length = $w \cdot 6w = 6w^2$. So $f(w)$ outputs the rectangle's area in square feet.

steps.title: Interpret the value $f(14) = 1{,}176$
steps.content: In $f(14) = 1,176$: the input $w = 14$ is the width in feet, and the output 1,176 is the area in ft². Therefore, when the width is 14 ft, the area is 1,176 ft². That matches choice A — A is correct.
